que: What does a coroutine dispatcher control?
options: Which thread or thread pool executes coroutine work

que: Which dispatcher is used for UI-related work on Android?
Which dispatcher is optimized for disk and network I/O?
Which dispatcher is a common choice for CPU-intensive work?

options: Moves blocking work off the main thread

que: Which elements can be part of a coroutine context?
options: Dispatcher, job, name, and exception handler

que: What is a common dispatcher mistake?
options: Using IO for blocking network requests
Using Main for quick UI updates

que: Why can dispatcher injection be useful?
options: It makes tests easier by allowing replacement with test dispatchers
It removes the need for scopes

que: What generally happens to coroutine context in child coroutines?
options: It is inherited from the parent with child-specific job handling

que: Which official docs best support this topic?
options: Android coroutines guide, advanced coroutines, and best practices
